name: Kahurangi 500
description: The best 500k of biking in New Zealand???
text1: The Kahurangi 500 is a huge backcountry circuit around Kahurangi National Park, combining the Old Ghost Road, Heaphy Track, Rāmeka Track and Porika Track with remote gravel roads.

This is a mountain-bike journey rather than a conventional gravel tour. Long stretches of Grade 3 and Grade 4 singletrack demand confident handling, particularly when the bike is fully loaded. We recommend plenty of suspension on your bike.
text2: The Old Ghost Road supplies some of the route’s most dramatic riding. Dense West Coast forest onto exposed ridgelines before dropping through steep switchbacks towards the Mōkihinui River.

The Heaphy offers an equally memorable journey, subtropical nīkau palms on the beach, tussock downs and native forest. It is a New Zealand Great Walk, and mountain bikes are permitted only from 1 May to 30 November. All huts on Heaphy and Old Ghost must be booked in advance.
The Rāmeka and Porika tracks add further steep, rough and often slippery riding.
text3: You can make this route a 600km lap starting and finishing from Nelson; however, most people start in Murchinson or Westport.
